actionable_feedback: Provide parseable exports of all critical workbook sheets as CSV (raw-data, calculation, sensitivity, remediation) so automated graders can inspect values and computations.

If formulas were used to compute any reported fields (stall_margin, gate_margin, costs, budget headroom), include the exact formula text and cell references in the memo (e.g., sheet name, cell range, formula expression) or include a plain-text file listing formulas.
Make the workbook formulas visible/unprotected (or provide a separate 'formulas' sheet) so reviewers can confirm provenance without proprietary libraries.

Clarify the intended semantics and expected domain of fifo_full (the memo marks non-binary values as 'needs_review' - explicitly state whether fifo_full should be binary, a percentage, or another unit and how to treat non-binary readings).

Add a short reproducibility section in the memo that lists the steps to re-run key calculations (e.g., which CSV maps to which calculations, any rounding/normalization rules, and the sensitivity perturbation method).
Include a CSV or text representation of any intermediate computed tables used to derive remediation feasibility (cost vs budget), so cost arithmetic can be validated independently.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Clear and well-structured. Contains rule statement, per-row table, sensitivity conclusion, and remediation feasibility table. Memo should include explicit formula expressions (or point to a parseable sheet) for values labelled stall_margin, gate_margin, and budget headroom.

ready_valid_checker_1.xlsx: Present but could not be parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl_unavailable). Provide CSV exports and/or a plain-text listing of formulas so verification can be completed.

Score was reduced because the grader could not parse the workbook (openpyxl / artifact tooling unavailable) and therefore could not verify spreadsheet formulas and cell-level provenance. Provide parseable representations or verbatim formula/provenance excerpts so reproducibility and auditable formulas can be confirmed.
